behavior_summary: U8P_ARMLEFT implements simple activation and U8P_ARMLEFT::hit-response behavior for the ARMLEFT body-part object.

U8P_ARMLEFT::use (a process) spawns a BODYPART routine (BODYPART::func0270) with argument 0 when the object is activated — likely to start an animation/effect or otherwise initialize the body-part behavior.

U8P_ARMLEFT::hit (a process taking parameters uword, word) first calls FREE::resetRef(0x0202, this) to clear a reference tied to this object, then queries Kernel::getNumProcesses(0x020A, this). If no processes of that type are running, it spawns BODYPART::func0431(0) to run a guarded U8P_ARMLEFT::hit-reaction routine, preventing duplicate U8P_ARMLEFT::hit reactions.
notable_control_flow: U8P_ARMLEFT::use immediately spawns an external BODYPART process unconditionally on activation.

U8P_ARMLEFT::hit contains a guard: it resets a reference, checks Kernel::getNumProcesses for type 0x020A on this object, and only spawns the U8P_ARMLEFT::hit reaction if the count is zero — effectively serializing or deduplicating U8P_ARMLEFT::hit reactions.
open_questions: What exact gameplay effects do BODYPART::func0270 and BODYPART::func0431 perform (animation, state change, or other visual/logic effect)? — likely animation/effect but not explicit.
What do the numeric process types (0x0207, 0x020A) map to in the engine's semantics (look/U8P_ARMLEFT::use/U8P_ARMLEFT::hit categories)?
What is the semantic meaning of U8P_ARMLEFT::hit(uword, word) parameters (attacker id, damage, U8P_ARMLEFT::hit flags, or direction)?
What is stored under the reference type 0x0202 that FREE::resetRef clears, and why must it be reset on U8P_ARMLEFT::hit?

main_function.name: U8P_ARMLEFT::use
main_function.purpose: Handle object activation by starting a body-part-specific routine (spawn BODYPART::func0270 with argument 0). This likely triggers an animation or effect associated with the left arm.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the ARMLEFT object is used/activated by the player or game logic.
Tied to process type 0x0207 (canonical execution data).

function_map.name: U8P_ARMLEFT::use
function_map.role: Activation handler that spawns BODYPART::func0270 to start the body-part routine

function_map.name: U8P_ARMLEFT::hit
function_map.role: Hit event handler that resets a reference and conditionally spawns a U8P_ARMLEFT::hit-reaction BODYPART routine (guarded to avoid duplicates)

function_map.name: BODYPART::func0270
function_map.role: External body-part routine spawned by U8P_ARMLEFT::use (likely animation/effect starter)

function_map.name: BODYPART::func0431
function_map.role: External U8P_ARMLEFT::hit-reaction routine spawned by U8P_ARMLEFT::hit when no active 0x020A processes exist

function_map.name: FREE::resetRef
function_map.role: Reference management call used in U8P_ARMLEFT::hit to clear/reset a reference with key 0x0202 for this object

function_map.name: Kernel::getNumProcesses
function_map.role: Engine query to count processes of a given type on this object (used to gate spawning of duplicate reactions)

external_calls.purpose: Start the body-part routine (animation/effect) for ARMLEFT on object activation with argument 0
Clear/reset reference keyed 0x0202 for this object to prepare for U8P_ARMLEFT::hit handling/cleanup
Check how many processes of type 0x020A are currently running on this object to avoid spawning duplicate U8P_ARMLEFT::hit-reaction processes
Start a U8P_ARMLEFT::hit-reaction body-part routine with argument 0 when no 0x020A processes are active

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_ARMLEFT implements simple activation and U8P_ARMLEFT::hit-response behavior for the ARMLEFT body-part object.
